title: MPXV Sequencing from Wastewater (ONT) V1.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a61ih","text":"This protocol is designed to sequence MPXV from wastewater samples using a limited amplicon scheme that targets all MPXV clades, subclades, and lineages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5a6vl","text":"Q5® High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix: M0492L, AMPure XP beads: A63882, Qubit High Sensitivity DNA kit: Q32851, D5000 ScreenTape: 5067-5589, D5000 Reagents: 5067-5588, Quick T4 DNA Ligase: 101640-840, NEBNext Ultra End Repair/dA-Tailing Module: E7546S, Invitrogen UltraPure Agarose: 16500500, 1X TAE Buffer (from 50X TAE stock): 1610743, Invitrogen 1 Kb Plus DNA Ladder: 10787018, Thermo Scientific 6X DNA Loading Dye: R0611, Gel electrophoresis system and power source, Thermo Scientific GeneJET Gel Extraction Kit: K0691, Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) (50 mg/ml): AM2616, Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit V14: SQK-LSK114, Oxford Nanopore Flow Cell R10.4.1: FLO-MIN114, MPXV_v1.0_primer sequences","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare the following master mix per sample (+10%): %s %s
Add 6 μl of PCR master mix to the 96-well PCR reaction plate.
Add 4 μl sample to the 96-well PCR plates. Mix by pipetting. Seal plates with foil seals and briefly spin down.
Run the following program on thermocycler: 1) 98°C for 30 sec 2) 95°C for 15 sec 3) 60°C for 5 min Repeat steps 2-3 for a total of 40 cycles 4) 4°C for ∞
Check the amplification product on the Tapestation with a D5000 ScreenTape. Expected fragment size ~225-275bp. Spot check a couple samples if running multiple.
In a PCR tube, add 1 μl of amplified PCR product to 5 μl of sample buffer. Add 1 μl of D5000 ladder to 5 μl of sample buffer.
Vortex for 1 min at 2000 rpm. Briefly spin before loading on the Tapestation.
Combine the tailed primers equimolarly and dilute to 4.1 µM (50 nM for each primer). *Each primer will be 2 nM per oligo final concentration in solution.
Aliquot 7 μl of the PCR master mix in a 96-well PCR reaction plate.
Prepare the following master mix per sample (+10%): %s %s
Add 90 µl H2O to the amplified DNA OR add 9 µl H2O to 1 µl amplified DNA to dilute it 10x. Mix by pipetting.

Run the following program on thermocycler: 1) 95°C for 5 min 2) 98°C for 30 sec 3) 60°C for 20 min 4) 72°C for 2 min Repeat steps 2-4 once more 5) 98°C for 30 sec 6) 65°C for 30 sec 7) 72°C for 2 min Repeat steps 5-7 14 more times* 8) 72°C for 5 min 9) 4°C for ∞ *The first part of this PCR ensures that the tailed primers can introduce the adapter overhangs. After that there will be 6 more cycles of PCR. For large batches, this can be lowered (6 total cycles).
Add 1 μl amplified PCR product (10x diluted) to the 96-well reaction plates. Mix by pipetting. Seal plates with foil seals and briefly spin down.
Add 1 μl of the unique NB reverse primer to each sample (10 µM; 1 µM final concentration).
Add 1 μl of the unique NB forward primer to each sample (10 µM; 1 µM final concentration).
Allow AMPure XP beads to equilibrate to room temperature, vortex until homogenous.
Discard supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Place the plate on a magnetic stand and wait until the solution appears clear.
Add 7 μl beads to 10 μl of the library PCR product. Mix well by pipetting and incubate at room temp for 10 minutes.
Leave the plate on the magnet to air dry for 3 min.
Repeat the previous 80% EtOH wash step and remove as much EtOH as possible. NOTE: use a P20 pipette tip to remove all excess EtOH.

With the plate on the magnet, add 200 μl of 80% EtOH, incubate for 30 seconds, and remove the EtOH wash. NOTE: do not resuspend the beads in the wash solution. Just make sure the wash covers the beads stuck to the side of the well.
Place the plate on the magnet. When the solution appears clear, remove supernatant without disturbing the beads and place into a new plate.
Remove the plate from the magnet. Add 20 μl of nuclease-free water and mix well by pipetting. Incubate at room temp for 10 minutes.
Add 10 μl Qubit HS DNA standard #1 to one standard Qubit tube.
Add 190 μl Qubit HS DNA buffer to a Qubit tube for each standard (2).
Add 199 μl Qubit HS DNA buffer to a Qubit tube for each sample.
Check the library product on the Tapestation with a D5000 ScreenTape. Expected fragment size ~400 bp. Spot check a couple samples if running multiple.
Quantify using the Qubit reader.
Transfer 1 μl library from library elution plate to corresponding. Mix well by pipetting.
Add 10 μl Qubit HS DNA standard #2 to other standard Qubit tube.
Calculate the molarity of the libraries.
Pool libraries equimolar to 50ul at 20nM (or highest concentration possible)
Prepare the following reaction in a PCR tube: %s %s
Run the following program on thermocycler: 1) 20°C for 30 min 2) 65°C for 30 min 3) 4°C for ∞
Make a 1.5% agarose gel with a well large enough to hold 70ul
Excise the band at around 400bp
Load the samples and run the gel for about 45 minutes at 110V
Prepare the sample and controls for running on a gel (for a 10x loading dye): Ladder: 9 uL ladder + 1 uL loading dye Sample: 60 uL + 6.6 uL loading dye
Extract it following the protocol from the gel extraction kit and elute in 30μl of water
Dilute A-tailed library pool to 9.0 nM
Combine the following in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube: %s %s
Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down. Incubate the reaction for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Add 0.4x (40 µl) of resuspended AMPure XP beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Determine DNA concentration of the ligated library pool using Qubit High Sensitivity DNA kit
Place the tube on a magnetic rack. Once the solution clears, elute into a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µl Elution Buffer (EB) by pipetting. Spin down and incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual supernatant. Allow to dry for ~30 seconds, but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking.
Repeat the previous step for a total of 2x washes.

Wash the beads by adding 250 μl Short Fragment Buffer (SFB). Flick the beads to resuspend, spin down, then return the tube to the magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet. Remove the supernatant using a pipette and discard.
Load sample according to ONT manufacturer’s instructions
creator: Andersen Lab
